executive_validation: The main conclusion is supported: a Bayesian decision-support layer is not yet justified, and DO_NOT_BUILD now plus OBSERVE_ONLY for UC-DL-01 is superior to building a probabilistic layer today. Corrections are required for baseline metadata, stale G0 wording, confidence calibration, and the heuristic nature of the proposed observation threshold.

integrity.personal_data_scan_result: NO_ROW_LEVEL_PERSONAL_DATA_DETECTED; ONLY POLICY TEXT ABOUT PERSONAL DATA WAS FOUND
integrity.baseline_metadata_note: The phase validates commit 1e08ac20c3a3a812bcb2790d01f097eb022d3cbb, but Claude's JSON/report fields state baseline_commit=e5eef29c5a8726f93438695ce0fb7e3987710886. This is documentary metadata drift, not a reversal of the substantive conclusion.

central_claims.claim: Current Inventory and Control Gestion surfaces are mostly deterministic.
evidence: app/screens/reposicion.py _row_indicadores uses fixed checks on Venta(+7), NEGATIVO, RIESGO DE QUIEBRE, and OTROS.
app/screens/control_gestion.py renders fixed ALERTA/CUMPLE/INCUMPLE surfaces from mart outputs.
research/C007_CANONICAL_BUILDER_VALIDATION.json reports deterministic repeated and clean-room canonical outputs.
central_claims.limits: No DB or app/db.py read was authorized in this phase, so this validates the allowed runtime surfaces and research artifacts only.

central_claims.claim: There is no functional implementation of decision_and_action_tracking today.
evidence: research/AI_CAPABILITY_MAP.json has decision_and_action_tracking with current_implementation=[] and implementation_authorized=false.
Allowed app screens inspected do not expose an action/outcome write workflow.

central_claims.claim: Architecture and phase decisions are poorly comparable and path-dependent.
evidence: research/AI_PROJECT_HORIZON.json defines dependency-gated H0-H5 horizons and Bastian decision points.
H1/H2/H3/H4 dependencies make phase selection a sequenced governance problem rather than an exchangeable repeated trial.
central_claims.limits: This is an architectural inference, not a measured statistical result.

central_claims.claim: Load reliability is the most repetitive and observable candidate surface.
evidence: research/AI_SHARED_MEMORY.json records cg_audit.batch_registry behavior and 37 CG batches.
scripts/refresh_control_gestion_v2_incremental.py exposes repeated dry-run/apply validation status surfaces.
central_claims.limits: No live DB recount was authorized.

central_claims.claim: There are too few observations and anomaly labels to justify a probabilistic layer now.
evidence: The repo memory supports small aggregate batch counts and no action/outcome ledger.
The reviewed artifacts do not identify a labeled bad/good batch corpus.
central_claims.limits: Exact per-source labeled anomaly counts require a future read-only DB/evidence phase; this phase could not inspect DB or heavy evidence.

central_claims.claim: There is insufficient evidence that Bayes would beat a rule or scorecard today.
evidence: No use case in research/BAYES_001_USE_CASE_MATRIX.json is marked BAYES_SUITABLE_NOW.
UC-DL-01 still needs labels and a deterministic fixed-band baseline.
UC-PA-01 and UC-PA-02 require absent action/outcome tracking before a probabilistic comparison is meaningful.

central_claims.claim: The personal-growth rail must remain separate and private.
evidence: research/BAYES_001_INDEPENDENT_ASSESSMENT.json sets a private boundary and states no personal data was written.
research/BAYES_001_USE_CASE_MATRIX.json keeps UC-PG-01 conceptual/private with no repository data capture.

alternatives.finding: The assessment gives Bayes and HYBRID a future role where uncertainty and feedback loops exist, while favoring rules/scorecards for current safety gates and deterministic surfaces. That weighting is proportionate to the evidence.
alternatives.correction: The report should explicitly say DO_NOT_BUILD means no probabilistic implementation now, not no future measurement or comparison.

criteria_duplication: outcome_observability, data_availability, and measurement_cost partly overlap
implementation_cost and measurement_cost partly overlap for uninstrumented product cases
values_without_direct_evidence: Exact 1-5 scores are judgement calls and not independently measured.
20-30 clean and 3-5 anomalous batch thresholds are reasonable heuristics, not statistically derived sample-size calculations.
use_case_matrix_audit.aggregation_bias: LOW_TO_MEDIUM; no total score was used as an automatic decision, but high-impact product cases still need narrative gating because action data is absent.
omitted_cases: A future downstream probability consumer in app/db.py was not checked because app/db.py was outside the allowed read list.
Exact historically labeled bad batches were not checked because DB and heavy evidence were prohibited.
probability_could_change_decision_later: UC-DL-01 if labeled batch outcomes accrue and a predictive band beats fixed bands
UC-PA-02 if action/outcome tracking plus privacy and causal design exist
UC-PA-03 only after a concrete consuming decision is defined

uc_dl_01.future_comparison_trigger: After normal operations produce per-source labeled batch outcomes, run a read-only retrospective comparison of fixed deterministic bands vs predictive bands at equal recall; require lower false-positive rate without missing labeled bad batches.

corrections_required: Correct Claude artifact baseline metadata or annotate why e5eef29 is the research baseline while 1e08ac20 is the commit under validation.
Replace stale G0 production-priority reference with H1/CG-005 active horizon wording.
Mark 20-30 clean and 3-5 anomalous observations as a heuristic trigger, not a proven sample-size threshold.
Preserve NO_PILOT_YET until deterministic fixed-band baseline and labels exist.

temporal_correction.finding: The report states production priority collides with active gates including G0 parity and CG-005. The live horizon is H1 with CG-005 active. The conclusion remains unchanged because implementation/production remain NONE_NOW.
